name: Implicit coupling between classes via private state and shared mutables

planted_issues.description: OrderProcessor.process_order directly accesses InventoryManager._internal_state (a private dict) to check availability, bypassing the public API. This creates tight coupling to internal implementation details that can break silently when InventoryManager refactors its internals.

Module-level mutable dict _service_config is read by both classes and mutated by OrderProcessor (line 85). This shared mutable state creates hidden coupling — one class's behavior changes unpredictably when the other modifies config at runtime.

Module body makes an HTTP call to the feature flag service on import (lines 21-23). This causes import-time side effects: the module fails to load if the flag service is unreachable, breaking test isolation and making cold starts fragile.

clean_areas.description: InventoryManager's public API methods (reserve, get_pending_shipments, sync_inventory) are well-designed with clear contracts, proper logging, and appropriate return types.
